channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: Iran's Exiled Crown Prince Reza Pahlavi Splattered With Red Liquid in Berlin
item.description: Iran's exiled Crown Prince Reza Pahlavi was splattered with red liquid after a news briefing in Berlin where he criticized the US-Iran ceasefire. The attack occurred as he left Germany's federal news conference building.

item.title: Trump Says Israel and Lebanon Agree to Extend Ceasefire by 3 Weeks
item.description: President Trump announced Israel and Lebanon agreed to extend the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire by three weeks after talks at the White House. The meeting between Israeli and Lebanese ambassadors was the second in a week, representing the first direct diplomatic talks in decades.

item.title: Millions of Americans May Now Qualify for Canadian Citizenship Under New Law
item.description: A recent change to Canada's citizenship requirements has led to a surge in applications from Americans. The new law, which took effect December 15, allows those with Canadian parents or grandparents to claim citizenship more easily.

item.title: Russian drone strike kills married couple, injures 15 in Odesa residential buildings
item.description: A Russian overnight drone attack on multiple residential buildings in Odesa killed two civilians and wounded 15 others, the latest in a series of strikes on southern Ukrainian cities.

item.title: Seven torture chambers holding Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant staff uncovered
item.description: Ukrainian human rights organization Truth Hounds has identified at least seven torture chambers used to detain staff of the occupied Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant, revealing systematic abuse of Ukrainian personnel.

item.title: US offers $4 million bounty for Chinese money launderer linked to crypto scams
item.description: The US State Department announced a $4 million reward for information leading to the arrest of Daren Li, a Chinese national who fled after admitting to laundering funds from cryptocurrency scams targeting victims across Southeast Asia.

item.title: Navy rushing Hellfire missiles to carrier groups for anti-drone defense
item.description: The US Navy is accelerating deployment of Hellfire missiles to carrier strike groups as the threat of one-way attack drones in the Middle East intensifies following Iranian attacks on shipping.

item.title: Iran war created biggest ever energy security threat, IEA warns
item.description: The International Energy Agency declared the US-Iran conflict has created the single largest threat to global energy security in history, with Strait of Hormuz disruptions sending shockwaves through oil markets worldwide.

item.title: Trump orders US military to "shoot and kill" Iranian boats laying mines in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump has ordered the Navy to destroy small Iranian boats deploying mines in the strategic waterway, tripling minesweeping operations as tensions escalate despite an ongoing ceasefire.

item.title: Ukraine emerges as international arms exporter, selling drone defense tech to Middle East
item.description: Ukraine has developed defense technology to counter Iranian drones similar to those Russia uses, and is now exporting these systems to countries facing similar threats.

item.title: Iran Dismisses Trump Leadership Rift Claims, Vows Iron Unity
item.description: Iranian officials reject Trump claims of internal division, emphasizing the "iron unity of the nation and government" amid ongoing conflict with the US.

item.title: US Soldier Charged After Betting $400,000 on Maduro Removal Using Classified Info
item.description: Gannon Ken Van Dyke allegedly made trades on Polymarket based on classified information about Venezuela operations, faces up to 60 years in prison.

item.title: Meta Cuts 8,000 Jobs as Microsoft Offers Buyouts Amid AI Spending Surge
item.description: Major tech layoffs continue as Meta announces 10% workforce reduction and Microsoft offers voluntary retirement to 7% of workers, both citing AI investments.

item.title: More Than 500 Killed in Tanzania Election Violence Inquiry Finds
item.description: Opposition parties accused security forces, but the official report does not attribute responsibility for the deaths during election unrest.

item.title: White House Memo Claims Mass AI Theft by Chinese Firms
item.description: A memo from Michael Kratsios alleges that firms, mainly in China, are wrongfully distilling US AI models, escalating tech tensions.

item.title: Trump ally to lead bipartisan delegation to China ahead of May summit
item.description: Republican senator will head to China on May 1 with stops in Shanghai and Beijing as the Trump administration increases pressure on Beijing amid the Iran crisis.

item.title: Finland says China-Russia ties mean EU trade deal is off the table
item.description: Finnish foreign minister says Helsinkis tough stance means a de facto veto on any EU-China trade agreement due to Beijings continued support for Moscow.

item.title: 12 Russian FSB officers killed in drone strike on command post in occupied Donetsk
item.description: Ukrainian forces used FP-2 drones with 60-100kg payloads to strike an FSB command post, killing 12 officers in a significant intelligence blow to Russian security services.

item.title: Ukraine first to scale remotely operated interceptor drones, defense minister says
item.description: Ukraine becomes the first country to scale the use of remotely operated interceptor drones capable of long-distance strikes, marking a significant advancement in drone warfare.

item.title: Ukrainian unmanned vessel launches interceptor to knock out Shahed drone
item.description: Ukraine has deployed a powerful new defense against Russian Shahed drones - an unmanned naval vessel that can launch interceptors, potentially blunting Russias ability to force concessions.

item.title: EU Approves $106 Billion Loan for Ukraine Military Support
item.description: The European Union approved a massive loan package heavily weighted toward military spending, signaling expectations of a prolonged conflict. Unlike previous aid packages, this one is designed to put Ukraine on solid footing for a long fight against Russia.

item.title: Iran Under Collective Military Leadership After Khamenei Death
item.description: Following the killing of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran has shifted to a collective leadership structure with the Revolutionary Guards wielding significantly more power. The new arrangement includes prominent generals and Mojtaba Khamenei as supreme leader.

item.title: Trump Says King Charles Visit Could Repair US-UK Relations
item.description: In a BBC interview, President Trump said the upcoming visit by King Charles III could "absolutely" help repair strained relations between the United States and United Kingdom. The president discussed next week's visit and his relationship with the UK Prime Minister.

item.title: BBC Analysis: Iranian Forces Staged Video of Ship Seizures
item.description: Video analysis indicates that parts of a propaganda video showing masked Iranian forces seizing ships appear to have been filmed hours after the vessels were reportedly captured. The revelation raises questions about Iran's information operations during the conflict.

item.title: Pope urges US and Iran to return to peace talks, condemns capital punishment
item.description: Pope Leo XIV called for de-escalation and a return to diplomatic negotiations between the US and Iran during his Africa tour, criticizing capital punishment while addressing the ongoing conflict.
